business.headline: PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year rattles jewellery marketsPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year has rattled jewellery markets, with traders warning that weaker demand could hurt small jewellers, karigars and thousands of workers dependent on the industry.
Zoho to bring back WFH? Sridhar Vembu responds to PM Modi's appealVembu said Zoho may revisit WFH after PM Modi urged fuel-saving measures to ease economic pressure.

Rs 17.3 lakh schooling, Rs 5.4 lpa salary: Is education becoming a financial trap?Education has long been treated as a non-negotiable expense in Indian households. But today, as costs rise year after year, more families are quietly asking a difficult question: is quality schooling becoming harder to afford?

Petrol, diesel prices may soon go up. How much more could you pay?Petrol and diesel prices could be hiked soon amid surging crude oil prices and escalating tensions in West Asia. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether the likely fuel price hikes would be sharp or gradual over a period of time.

India's best defence is to electrify everything, says Sagar AdaniSagar Adani said India's long-term constraints, water security, food systems, and digital growth, all run into the same bottleneck: reliable, affordable energy.

No petrol or diesel shortage, govt assures day after PM's 'save fuel' appealDuring an inter-ministerial briefing on the situation in West Asia, government officials said India has enough fuel stocks and has taken steps to reduce the impact of the ongoing conflict in the region.

IT employees' union urges govt to back work-from-home following PM's appealIn a letter dated May 11, 2026, NITES urged the Ministry of Labour and Employment to direct IT and digitally deliverable service sectors to implement work-from-home arrangements for a suitable period in the national interest.

Sensex ends 1,313 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; Titan down 7%The BSE Sensex plunged 1,312.91 points, or 1.70%, to close at 76,015.28, while the Nifty 50 fell 360.30 points, or 1.49%, to settle at 23,815.85, slipping below the key 24,000 mark.

Government has no plans to raise gold, silver import duties: ReportThe clarification comes a day after PM Modi urged citizens to reduce fuel consumption, avoid unnecessary foreign travel and postpone gold purchases as India prepares for the economic impact of rising oil prices linked to the Iran conflict and fears surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.

May 2026 tax deadlines: These key TDS, TCS dates taxpayers should not missIf you have tax-related work pending, May 2026 is a month you may not want to ignore. Several key TDS and TCS deadlines are approaching, and missing them could lead to late fees, penalties or unnecessary stress.

8th Pay Commission: Why govt employees want family unit revised from 3 to 5As part of its continuing exclusive and in-depth coverage of the 8th Pay Commission, IndiaToday.in has learnt that employee bodies are now pushing for the current '3-family-unit' formula to be revised to '5 family units'.

What happens if Indians stop buying gold for a year like PM Modi asked?Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to postpone non-essential gold purchases amid rising oil prices and West Asia tensions. Economists say any sustained fall in demand could ease pressure on the current account deficit, rupee and forex reserves.

PM Modi's call hits gold prices: Should investors worry?For years, gold has been the go-to safe investment for Indian families during uncertain times. But after PM Modi's call to cut back on gold buying, prices took a hit. As global worries rise and the yellow metal weakens, should investors start worrying?

The math behind PM Modi's gold, petrol and foreign travel advisoryPM Modi's latest advisory is not just about cutting fuel use or avoiding gold purchases. It is really about India's current account deficit, rising crude oil prices and protecting the economy from external shocks. Here is why your fuel bill, gold jewellery and foreign trip suddenly matter more than you think.

Titan shares fall over 6%: Why jewellery stocks are under pressure todayTitan and other jewellery stocks fell sharply after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged people to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The remark added to concerns over gold demand, imports and pressure on the rupee amid elevated crude prices.

Sensex crashes 1,300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.
Sensex plunges 800 points at open as crude oil jumps on US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 847.62 points to 76,480.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 249.65 points to 23,926.50 as of 9:27 am.

Will stock market crash today after PM Modi's warning on fuel and gold buying?Markets are expected to react to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's remarks asking citizens to conserve fuel and avoid buying gold for weddings for one year amid pressure from rising global energy prices.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayOnce upon a time, Rs 1 crore felt like enough to secure life after retirement. Today, many Indians believe even Rs 10–15 crore may not be enough. I spoke to families to find out what retirement means to them today, and why expectations are rising.

entertainment.headline: Jana Nayagan producer opens up on censor issues delaying Vijay's film release Jana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana said the revising committee has watched the film and the team is awaiting the final censor certificate. The delay has kept Vijay's final film on hold and affected its release plans.

Vijay is an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu: Jana Nayagan producer Jana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana said Vijay could grow into a strong administrator as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. More than an actor or a politician, he said Vijay was an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu.

Celina Jaitly says husband manipulated Austrian laws to block her access to sons Celina Jaitly has alleged that her husband used Austrian laws to cut off her access to their sons. The claim comes alongside her earlier court case seeking relief, damages and compensation for alleged abuse and financial loss.

BTS concert turns Mexico City into fan festival as 40,000 gather outside stadium BTS's second Mexico City concert drew massive crowds as videos from the show spread online. The scenes underscored the group's strong fan bond and the scale of interest around their return.

Amitabh Bachchan gives special instructions to security team for fans Amitabh Bachchan said he has instructed his security team to treat fans with care and respect during his Sunday gatherings outside Jalsa. In his latest blog post, he said those waiting for hours in the heat deserve goodwill and proper behaviour.

Alia Bhatt arrives for Cannes 2026 in Carolina Herrera ensemble. Watch video Alia Bhatt arrived in Nice ahead of the Cannes Film Festival, turning heads in a sharply tailored all-black look featuring luxury designer labels and statement accessories.

Ram Charan's intense new Peddi poster creates buzz ahead of trailer launch The makers of Peddi have unveiled a striking new poster of Ram Charan while announcing the trailer release date, adding to the growing anticipation around the upcoming film.

Diljit Dosanjh on Calgary concert row: I won't let fans be harassed at my show Diljit Dosanjh broke his silence on the disruption at his Calgary, Canada, concert, saying he would not allow anyone to harass fans or disturb his shows. In his note, the singer said the controversy was about people's intention, rather than the flags.

Miami cops sue Ben Affleck and Matt Damon over alleged reputation damage in The Rip Miami-Dade officers Jason Smith and Jonathan Santana have sued Ben Affleck and Matt Damon's firm over The Rip. They claim that the film's framing as a true story, combined with its depiction of fictional conduct, has damaged their reputations.

Mohanlal on Vijay's superstardom: There is an unknowable magic During the promotions of Drishyam 3, Mohanlal spoke about Vijay's charisma and the magic that helped him connect with audiences. His remarks came as Vijay assumed office as the Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu following Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam's sweeping 2026 poll win.

Arjun Kapoor celebrates 14 years of Ishaqzaade: Parma changed everything for me Arjun Kapoor marked 14 years in Bollywood by revisiting Ishaqzaade on Instagram. His post, featuring sketches from the film, renewed attention on the role that launched his career.

Tovino Thomas, Nazriya reveal new film title amid Barcelona vs Real Madrid clash Malayalam actors Tovino Thomas and Nazriya Nazim unveiled Gracias El Clasico in Barcelona during El Clasico. The launch, which took place during the Barcelona vs Real Madrid football match, marks their first film together and promises a blend of love, family dynamics and football.

Anupam Kher revisits iconic DDLJ shooting spot, makes special appeal to government Anupam Kher returned to Apta Railway Station and revisited the iconic Dilwale Dhulaniya Le Jayengey shooting spot after 32 years. He said the visit felt frozen in time and urged its preservation as cinematic heritage.

Who is Siddharth Gupta, actor playing Lord Krishna in Krishnavataram Part 1? Siddharth Gupta has grabbed the spotlight with his portrayal of Lord Krishna in Krishnavataram Part 1: The Heart. Here's all you need to know about the actor.

Director Mudassar Aziz defends Pati Patni Aur Woh Do amid infidelity criticism Director Mudassar Aziz has rejected criticism that Pati Patni Aur Woh Do trivialises extramarital affairs and promotes infidelity. He said the sequel centres on marriage, misunderstandings and a husband without a roving eye.

Remarkably Bright Creatures author on Netflix adaptation, Sally Field, Lewis Pullman Author Shelby Van Pelt said seeing her novel, Remarkably Bright Creatures, become a Netflix film feels surreal. She said the screen version works because it keeps the novel's emotional core intact.

Manjummel Boys fame Chidambaram locks June release for Balan: The Boy Chidambaram's Balan: The Boy is set for a June 2026 theatrical release, in five languages. The announcement comes just after its Cannes market screening was confirmed, adding momentum to the emotional drama.

Vijay and Udhayanidhi's old pic goes viral as they get set for Assembly face-off Vijay and Udhayanidhi Stalin have been film industry colleagues for years. They will now face off in the Assembly, with TVK's Vijay as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and Udhaynidhi as Leader of Opposition. An old photograph of the two actors-turned-politicians has now gone viral.

Abhishek Bachchan reacts to reports about actor fees for Raja Shivaji Abhishek Bachchan said reports about him charging for Raja Shivaji were false and stressed that his appearance was rooted in belief. He said the film mattered because of his bond with Riteish and Genelia Deshmukh and their approach beyond box office figures.

general news.headline: Save fuel, WFH, online classes, no gold: PM repeats appeal amid West Asia crisisIn Vadodra, PM Modi once again urged citizens to cut fuel use, postpone gold purchases, adopt work-from-home practices and avoid unnecessary foreign spending amid economic uncertainty caused by tensions and supply disruptions in West Asia.

Why TVK's White Paper could be a red flag for CM VijayAs C Joseph Vijay took oath as the chief minister of Tamil Nadu, he highlighted the state's debt burden of Rs 10 lakh crore and promised a White Paper. From the very stage, he also signed an executive order on free electricity of up to 200 units. Will the TVK's White Paper act as a guardrail for Vijay's poll promises?

Will regret setting foot on our soil: Iran official warns US against ground opIran's foreign ministry spokesperson warned the US against any military move and accused Washington of wrecking nuclear talks. The remarks come as tensions around the Strait of Hormuz rise and India prepares to host Iran's foreign minister for BRICS talks.

They voted for whistle: Vijay visits Vaiko, his domestic helps can't keep calmA moment from the visit, captured on camera, also showed three members of Vaiko's household staff having what appeared to be a clear fan-girl moment with actor-turned-politician Vijay.

Bengali Shaadi Mein Abdulla DeewanaThe despondency of the Non-Resident Bengalis or NRBs doesn't get reflected in the celebrations in West Bengal. The state no longer sticks out like a special sore thumb.

1% chance of living: Trump says Iran ceasefire on massive lifesupportDonald Trump dismissed Iran's latest proposal and said the US-Iran ceasefire was on massive life support. His remarks sharpened concerns over the Strait of Hormuz, sanctions demands and the fate of wider talks.

Avoid destination weddings abroad: PM's 'wed in India' push amid austerity callPM Modi urged Indians to hold weddings and holidays within the country instead of going abroad. The appeal tied personal spending choices to conserving foreign exchange during the West Asia-linked global disruption.

PM Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year rattles jewellery marketsPrime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to avoid buying gold for a year has rattled jewellery markets, with traders warning that weaker demand could hurt small jewellers, karigars and thousands of workers dependent on the industry.

Vijay is an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu: Jana Nayagan producerJana Nayagan producer Venkat K Narayana said Vijay could grow into a strong administrator as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister. More than an actor or a politician, he said Vijay was an emotion for the people of Tamil Nadu.

Bhopal land is bought by 50 IAS, IPS officers. A bypass project surges plot valueA group of nearly 50 IAS and IPS officers bought farmland near a village in Madhya Pradesh's Bhopal in 2022 through a single transaction. Within two years, the approval of a Rs 3,200-crore bypass project transforms it into a high-value real estate asset, according to a report.

ED arrests TMC leader and ex-minister Sujit Bose in municipal recruitment scamAccording to the Enforcement Directorate (ED), Bose had allegedly recommended around 150 candidates for various posts under the South Dum Dum Municipality in exchange for monetary benefits.

Shashi Tharoor claps back after Annamalai takes 'chameleon' swipe at CongressAt the Stanford India Conference 2026, K Annamalai called Congress a chameleon for backing Vijay's TVK after leaving the DMK. Shashi Tharoor responded with a wordplay on X, turning the exchange into a viral moment amid Tamil Nadu's government formation.

Indian-origin entrepreneur, wife clear loans of 176 US grads. Who's Anil Kochhar?At North Carolina State University's Wilson College, Indian-origin entrepreneur Anil Kochhar and his wife Marilyn stunned graduates by announcing they would clear the final-year student loans of the entire Class of 2026, honouring Anil's late father. The announcement drew roaring applause from both students and their families.

Live: Day after rejecting Iran's proposal, Trump says ceasefire on life supportUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Qatar condemned a drone attack on a cargo vessel in its territorial waters, warning that the strike threatens maritime security and commercial shipping in the Gulf amid rising tensions linked to the Iran war. The incident has heightened fears over the safety of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route. Meanwhile, Iran has reportedly sent its response to a US proposal to end the war through Pakistan-mediated diplomacy. Talks are currently focused on halting hostilities and ensuring safe passage through Hormuz. Iran also warned that any deployment of US, French or British warships in the strait would escalate the crisis and provoke an immediate response. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Trump gala attack accused Cole Allen pleads not guiltyCole Allen pleaded not guilty in Washington to charges linked to an alleged attempt on Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. The hearing also sharpened a defence challenge over whether senior officials who attended the event should be involved in the prosecution.

Celina Jaitly says husband manipulated Austrian laws to block her access to sonsCelina Jaitly has alleged that her husband used Austrian laws to cut off her access to their sons. The claim comes alongside her earlier court case seeking relief, damages and compensation for alleged abuse and financial loss.

The voice of the heartland: How Bhojpuri found its home in the IPLIPL 2026: Robin Singh has become one of the biggest faces of Bhojpuri commentary in the IPL. But beyond the microphone, the former coach and talent scout is also helping Bihar's next generation of cricketers find a path to the biggest stage.

Work From Home in India: Will corporates consider?Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call to work from home where possible has pushed Indian companies back into a remote-work debate. The discussion now centres on whether businesses are culturally and operationally ready for a lasting hybrid model.

TMC claims SIR deletions impacted Bengal poll results, Supreme Court respondsThe Supreme Court said Mamata Banerjee and others could file fresh applications over electoral roll deletions in West Bengal. The hearing centred on whether Special Intensive Revision disputes may have affected election results.

Folded hands, hugs and bouquets: Vijay meets ex-Tamil Nadu CM MK StalinA day after taking oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, TVK chief C Joseph Vijay met former CM MK Stalin and Udhayanidhi in Chennai ahead of the crucial May 13 Assembly floor test.

'Common man' Vijay swaps veshti-shirt for blazer and pants at oathVijay's attire and messaging marked a political turning point in Tamil Nadu, symbolising a generational shift and the end of an era of Dravidian political norms. With Arvind Kejriwal's "aam aadmi" image recently under strain amid controversies, Vijay appears to have filled the vacuum.

BSF to get full control of border fencing in 45 days: Bengal CM at 1st Cabinet meetIn his first Cabinet meeting, West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari announced that the land transfer process to the BSF to complete fencing the Bangladesh border will be done in 45 days. Adhikari also announced the full implementation of central schemes in the state.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy gold for a yearPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Two leaves, two factions: AIADMK battles for survival in Tamil Nadu resetSeparate arrivals by AIADMK MLAs at the new Assembly session underlined the challenge to Edappadi Palaniswami's leadership after another defeat. The unrest has revived talk of a split, with Vijay's TVK poised to gain from any rupture.

sports.headline: Axar Patel, David Miller redemption party keeps DC afloat in Dharamshala thriller IPL 2026: Axar Patel and David Miller finally delivered when Delhi Capitals needed them the most. Their fearless counterattack helped DC pull off a record chase in Dharamshala and keep their fading IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive.

Rahul Dravid enters European T20 Premier league, buys Dublin Guardians franchise Rahul Dravid has joined ETPL as owner of the Dublin Guardians before the league's 2026 launch. His move underlines Europe's latest attempt to build a serious franchise T20 market.

The voice of the heartland: How Bhojpuri found its home in the IPL IPL 2026: Robin Singh has become one of the biggest faces of Bhojpuri commentary in the IPL. But beyond the microphone, the former coach and talent scout is also helping Bihar's next generation of cricketers find a path to the biggest stage.

Tim David fined for obscene gesture during RCB's last-ball thriller vs MI Tim David was fined and given two demerit points after an obscene gesture during RCB's win over Mumbai Indians. The sanction capped a chaotic last-ball contest that boosted Bengaluru's playoff push and hurt Mumbai's chances.

25-0: Jannik Sinner keeps cooking before French Open with another historic feat Jannik Sinner crushed Alexei Popyrin 6-2, 6-0 at the Italian Open to extend his unbeaten Masters 1000 streak to 30 matches and become the first player to start 25-0 across the season's first five Masters events.

Neymar's FIFA World Cup 2026 dream gets major boost with Brazil squad return Carlo Ancelotti has included Neymar in Brazil's preliminary 55-man squad after his return with Santos. The call-up puts him back in contention for the 2026 World Cup, provided he stays fit and maintains form.

Salman Agha challenges Bangladesh in Mirpur Test: Set Pakistan 260 if brave enough BAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Salman Agha has challenged Bangladesh to set a target of around 260 in 70 overs, insisting Pakistan would go all out for victory in the opening Test at Mirpur.

Who is Madhav Tiwari? DC's fast-bowling all-rounder who troubled Priyansh Arya Young fast-bowling all-rounder Madhav Tiwari announced himself in IPL 2026 with the prized wicket of Priyansh Arya during Delhi Capitals' crucial clash against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala on Monday.

India's FIFA U17 World Cup dream ends after 0-3 loss to China in Asian Cup QF India's historic FIFA U17 Women's World Cup dream came to an end after a quarterfinal defeat to hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup. Despite the loss, the Young Tigresses created history by reaching the knockout stage for the first time.

Coco Gauff escapes huge scare before French Open with unreal Rome comeback Italian Open 2026: Defending French Open champion Coco Gauff saved a match point before storming back to beat teenage sensation Iva Jovic in a dramatic clash in Rome. The American rallied from 5-7, 3-5, 30-40 down to keep her Roland Garros preparations on track.

Kuldeep Yadav among 5 dropped by DC for PBKS clash as playoff hopes fade away IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals dropped Kuldeep Yadav and made five changes for the Punjab Kings match in Dharamshala. The decision exposed Delhi's tactical reset as Punjab tried to steady their top-four push.

Liverpool honour Diogo Jota and his brother with new Anfield memorial sculptureLiverpool FC have announced a permanent tribute to late forward Diogo Jota and his brother Andre Silva at Anfield, honouring the two Portuguese brothers who died in a car accident last year.

Ex-CSK star defends viral Krunal Pandya cramp comment, cites Tamil slangIPL 2026: S. Badrinath drew backlash over a Tamil commentary remark as Ryan Rickelton helped a cramping Krunal Pandya during RCB's chase against MI in Raipur. The former India cricketer said his comments were taken out of context.

England exploring historic white-ball tour of NepalEngland could become the biggest international side to tour Nepal after the ECB reportedly explored the possibility of a short T20I series during the 2027-31 FTP cycle.

Najmul Shanto stands tall on rain-hit day as Mirpur Test heads for thrilling finishBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Day 4 of the Mirpur Test was hit by rain interruptions, but Najmul Hossain Shanto's unbeaten 58 helped Bangladesh extend their lead to 179 against Pakistan.

Virat Kohli rates Krunal Pandya's fearless knock vs MI his best ever in IPLIPL 2026: Virat Kohli hailed Krunal Pandya's fighting 73 against Mumbai Indians as one of the best pressure knocks he has seen in the IPL. The RCB star praised Krunal's courage after the all-rounder battled severe cramps to guide Bengaluru to a thrilling win.

PBKS vs DC Highlights: Delhi keep play-off hopes alive with 3 wicket win over PBKSPBKS (Punjab) vs DC (Delhi) Highlights, IPL 2026: DC (216/7 in 19 ov) in chase of 211. Delhi Capitals middle-order finally stood up as they chased down a mammoth 200-plus as they hand PBKS their fourth successive loss.

India retain top spot in ICC men's ODI ranking after annual updateIndia held on to the top spot in the ICC Men's ODI team rankings after the annual update, despite slipping by one rating point. The reshuffle also sharpened the World Cup 2027 qualification race, with South Africa's host status making the cut-off more consequential.

South Africa pacer reverses retirement, to be named in Women's T20 World Cup squadVeteran South Africa fast bowler Shabnim Ismail is set to reverse her international retirement and return to the Proteas squad for the Women's T20 World Cup after discussions with team management.

Marcus Rashford to return to Man United? Barca winger drops hint after title triumphMarcus Rashford drove Barcelona towards the title against Real Madrid and then said he would stay if the choice were his. His remarks further point to a permanent split from Manchester United after a season that has reset his standing in Spain.

Vinesh Phogat hits back at Wrestling Federation over comeback controversyVinesh Phogat has publicly responded to WFI's showcause notice, claiming the federation wrongly interpreted her comeback timeline. The wrestler insisted she remains eligible to compete and plans to return to action at the National Open Ranking Tournament in Gonda.

Victory scar: Krunal flaunts pic of bruised stomach after heroic knock vs MIIPL 2026: Krunal Pandya battled cramps and blows to make 73 off 46 as RCB chased 167 against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. His innings, marked by pain, poise and defiance, sealed a crucial last-over win for RCB on Sunday.

Vinesh Phogat contests federation ban, arrives for comeback wrestling eventDespite the WFI declaring her ineligible, Vinesh Phogat arrived in Gonda for the National Open Ranking tournament and defended her comeback by citing compliance with regulations of word bodies.

George Bailey dismisses concerns after big-name Australian players reject contractsAustralia chair of selectors George Bailey played down concerns over a group of senior players rejecting Cricket Australia's initial contract offers, saying tensions around deals and franchise opportunities are "normal" in the current cricket landscape.

Why are all cricket finals going to Ahmedabad?The BCCI announced the venue for the Indian Premier League 2026 final. No prizes for guessing, that the match was granted to the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, much to the dissatisfaction of many fans.

Krunal excited about Kohli's slump? RCB star explains bizarre statementKrunal Pandya dismissed concerns over Virat Kohli's two-game run drought and backed him to hit back hard. Krunal said that there is no worry in the RCB camp about Kohli's form.

Peter Schmeichel accuses Arsenal of hypocrisy after controversial VAR callPeter Schmeichel blasted the officials after Arsenal's controversial 1-0 win over West Ham United, claiming the league leaders "would never be top" if similar physical challenges were consistently punished as fouls.

Delhi Capitals accept defeat in play-off race, to act party poopers for othersIPL 2026: Ian Bell says Delhi Capitals want to finish strongly despite an inconsistent season damaging their playoff push. Delhi will be aiming to spoil the party for the rest of the teams as the season comes to a close.

Shahbaz Ahmed leads big-money buys at Bengal T20 League auctionShahbaz Ahmed emerged as the biggest buy at the Bengal T20 League Men's Auction after Shrachi Tribes Rarh Tigers retained the Indian all-rounder for INR 12.20 lakh through the Right To Match card.

How MS Dhoni's advice turned CSK's Urvil Patel into an IPL record breakerUrvil Patel said a dressing-room conversation with MS Dhoni helped him reset before his 13-ball fifty against LSG. The knock powered CSK past LSG and doubled as a tribute to his father, who told him to bat freely.

No IPL stars in Australia squad for Pakistan ODIs, some return for Bangladesh tourCricket Australia named separate white-ball squads for Pakistan and Bangladesh under Mitchell Marsh. Pat Cummins, Mitchell Starc and Josh Hazlewood will miss both the tours, which get underway from May 30.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
